title: zaluzianskya dentata
intro: Zaluzianskya dentata, also known as the night-scented catchfly, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a biennial or perennial plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family and is characterized by its white or pink flowers with a strong, sweet fragrance that is released at night. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Eastern European and Asian cultures, due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Zaluzianskya dentata include treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions, and it can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or salve for these purposes.
